summary: Yahoo Finance / BeInCrypto reported Standard Chartered will cut more than 15% of corporate function roles by 2030 as the bank scales up artificial intelligence and automation, with reporting from The Guardian putting the reduction at about 7,800 redundancies.
evidenceQuote: Standard Chartered will cut more than 15% of corporate function roles by 2030 as the UK-headquartered bank scales up the use of artificial intelligence.
notes: The company framed the reductions as part of a strategy update and profitability push, with CEO Bill Winters saying the headcount reduction would be driven by greater use of AI and automation and describing it as replacing some lower-value human capital with financial and investment capital. The Guardian reported 15% of back-office roles would amount to about 7,800 redundancies out of more than 52,000 staff in those roles; the Yahoo Finance / BeInCrypto article described the plan as over 7,000 positions from a workforce of 80,000. Because AI and automation are explicitly cited as drivers, this is classified as explicit_ai_cited.

sources.name: Yahoo Finance / BeInCrypto
sources.quote: Standard Chartered will cut more than 15% of corporate function roles by 2030 as the UK-headquartered bank scales up the use of artificial intelligence.

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.quote: StanChart said on Tuesday it would cut 15% of its back-office roles by 2030, which would result in about 7,800 redundancies out of its more than 52,000 staff in such roles.
